expectations.evidence: run-parallel.sh is a complete bash script with shebang (#!/usr/bin/env bash), set -euo pipefail, functions, and a main entrypoint. It is executable and designed for parallel test execution across 5 groups.

The script uses 'go test ./internal/provider -v -count=1 -timeout 20m -run REGEX' directly for each group. There is no 'go test -c' step to pre-compile a test binary before launching groups. Each of the 5 groups compiles separately.

The script defines exactly 5 parallel groups (GROUP1 through GROUP5) with names job-wait-wf-ds-eda, job-disappears-inv-ds-wf, inv-group-jt-ds-actions, host-retry-org-ds-wf-inv, and wf-disappears-host-eda.

Job-launching tests are mixed with CRUD-only and data source tests across all groups. For example, Group 3 contains both CRUD tests (TestAccInventoryResource, TestAccGroupResource) and job-launching tests (TestAccAAPJob_UpdateWithSameParameters, TestAccAAPJobAction_failIgnore). The script explicitly uses bin-packing by duration rather than domain separation.

The script has no retry logic at all. When a group fails, it logs the failure and reports the exit code but does not retry. There is no exponential backoff or any backoff mechanism for timeout-related failures.

All 39 required acceptance tests are present in the group regexes with no gaps and no extras. Verified by extracting all test names from GROUP1-GROUP5 regexes and comparing against the full required list. The unique count matches exactly 39.

The script uses standard 'go test' flags (-v, -count=1, -timeout, -run) rather than pre-compiled binary flags (-test.run, -test.count=1, -test.timeout, -test.v). Since there is no pre-compiled binary step, the -test.* flag format is not used.

The script outputs sequential baseline (824s), estimated parallel time (~164s), and calculates actual speedup ratio (824/elapsed). Lines 152-155 print the estimates before running, and lines 206-213 print actual wall-clock time with computed speedup after completion.
